---
title: "Feature matrix"
subtitle: "Coverage of the ggplot2 and plotnine surface in Gribouille."
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
engine: markdown
---

## Context

Gribouille is a Typst package that implements a grammar of graphics on top of CeTZ.
This page is the canonical coverage table: one row per public ggplot2 symbol, using the [ggplot2 reference index](https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/reference/index.html) as the axis.
Status reflects what is currently in the package; Priority orders the remaining work.
The execution order, agent topology, and slice protocol live in the development roadmap (kept under `.claude/dev-plans/` in the repository).

Legend for **Status**:

- ✅ implemented.
- 🟡 partial or stubbed.
- ⬜ missing.
- ❌ out of scope for a static Typst backend (reason in Notes).

Legend for **Priority** (used to order future work):

- P1 unlocks common plots and blocks other work.
- P2 broadens expressiveness once P1 lands.
- P3 nice-to-have or niche.
- P4 deferred to the very end of the roadmap: requires a wasm helper or heavy numerical code that Typst cannot express natively. **Wasm is a last resort**, added only if a P4 feature is really needed and no native workaround exists. Until then every P4 row sits behind everything in P1 to P3.
- ∅ not planned.

## How gribouille currently maps to ggplot2

| Concept           | ggplot2 form                                      | gribouille form                                                                     |
| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Plot constructor  | `ggplot(data, aes(...)) + layer + scale + theme`. | `plot(data:, mapping:, layers:, scales:, coord:, facet:, theme:, width:, height:)`. |
| Layer addition    | `+ geom_point()`.                                 | `layers: (geom-point(), ...)`.                                                      |
| Aesthetic mapping | `aes(x = a, y = b, colour = c)`.                  | `aes(x: "a", y: "b", colour: "c")`.                                                 |
| Stat override     | `stat = "bin"` on a geom.                         | `stat:` key on the layer dict, dispatched via `src/stat/apply.typ`.                 |
| Theme composition | `theme_minimal() + theme(...)`.                   | `theme: theme-minimal()` (dict merged by `merge-theme`).                            |

## Feature matrix

### Plot basics

| ggplot2                   | gribouille symbol | Status | Priority | Notes                                                            |
| ------------------------- | ----------------- | ------ | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `ggplot()`                | `plot(...)`.      | ✅     | —        | —                                                                |
| `aes()`                   | `aes(...)`.       | ✅     | —        | Covers `x`, `y`, `colour`, `fill`, `size`, `alpha`, `group`.     |
| `%+%` / `add_gg()`        | —                 | ❌     | ∅        | Typst has no lazy plot object; composition is via function args. |
| `ggsave()`                | —                 | ❌     | ∅        | Typst owns the output pipeline.                                  |
| `qplot()` / `quickplot()` | —                 | ❌     | ∅        | Deprecated upstream.                                             |

### Geoms

| ggplot2                                                             | gribouille symbol                                     | Status | Priority | Notes                                                                                                                   |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- | ------ | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `geom_point()`                                                      | `geom-point`.                                         | ✅     | —        | —                                                                                                                       |
| `geom_line()`                                                       | `geom-line`.                                          | ✅     | —        | X-sorted connection of observations.                                                                                    |
| `geom_path()`                                                       | `geom-path`.                                          | ✅     | —        | Connects rows in input order.                                                                                           |
| `geom_step()`                                                       | `geom-step`.                                          | ✅     | —        | Stair-step interpolation; `direction` is `"hv"` (default) or `"vh"`.                                                    |
| `geom_col()`                                                        | `geom-col`.                                           | ✅     | —        | Honours `position = "stack"`/`"dodge"`/`"fill"`.                                                                        |
| `geom_bar()`                                                        | `geom-bar`.                                           | ✅     | —        | Wraps `geom-col` with `stat = "count"` and `position = "stack"`.                                                        |
| `geom_histogram()`                                                  | `geom-histogram`.                                     | ✅     | —        | Uses `stat-bin`.                                                                                                        |
| `geom_freqpoly()`                                                   | `geom-freqpoly`.                                      | ✅     | —        | Line through `stat-bin` midpoints; `bins` and `binwidth` mirror `geom-histogram`.                                       |
| `geom_ribbon()`                                                     | `geom-ribbon`.                                        | ✅     | —        | —                                                                                                                       |
| `geom_area()`                                                       | `geom-area`.                                          | ✅     | —        | Filled polygon between `y = 0` and `y` along x; pulls the y-domain to include zero.                                     |
| `geom_smooth()`                                                     | `geom-smooth`.                                        | 🟡     | P3       | `method = "lm"` only; `"loess"` deferred, wasm is last resort.                                                          |
| `geom_boxplot()`                                                    | `geom-boxplot`.                                       | ✅     | —        | Default `stat = "boxplot"` runs the per-group five-number summary; raw observations in, full box+whiskers+outliers out. |
| `geom_violin()`                                                     | —                                                     | ⬜     | P4       | Needs kernel density; deferred, wasm is last resort.                                                                    |
| `geom_density()` / `geom_density_2d()` / `geom_density_2d_filled()` | —                                                     | ⬜     | P4       | KDE; deferred, wasm is last resort.                                                                                     |
| `geom_dotplot()`                                                    | —                                                     | ⬜     | P4       | Niche.                                                                                                                  |
| `geom_jitter()`                                                     | `geom-jitter`.                                        | ✅     | —        | Point wrapper that defaults `position` to `"jitter"`.                                                                   |
| `geom_rug()`                                                        | `geom-rug`.                                           | ✅     | —        | Tick marks at observations on selected sides (`b`/`t`/`l`/`r`).                                                         |
| `geom_text()`                                                       | `geom-text`.                                          | ✅     | —        | Reads the `label` aesthetic.                                                                                            |
| `geom_label()`                                                      | `geom-label`.                                         | ✅     | —        | Boxed text with fill, stroke, radius.                                                                                   |
| `geom_rect()` / `geom_tile()` / `geom_raster()`                     | `geom-rect`, `geom-tile`, `geom-raster`.              | ✅     | —        | `geom-raster` is an alias of `geom-tile`.                                                                               |
| `geom_polygon()`                                                    | `geom-polygon`.                                       | ✅     | —        | One closed polygon per group; fill via `fill` or scale.                                                                 |
| `geom_segment()`                                                    | `geom-segment`.                                       | ✅     | —        | Maps `x`/`y`/`xend`/`yend`.                                                                                             |
| `geom_curve()`                                                      | —                                                     | ⬜     | P3       | Curved variant of segment; deferred until a real use case appears.                                                      |
| `geom_errorbar()`                                                   | `geom-errorbar`.                                      | ✅     | —        | Vertical range with horizontal end caps. `geom_errorbarh` deferred.                                                     |
| `geom_linerange()` / `geom_crossbar()` / `geom_pointrange()`        | `geom-linerange`, `geom-crossbar`, `geom-pointrange`. | ✅     | —        | linerange has no caps, crossbar is a filled box with a thick median bar, pointrange is a marker plus linerange.         |
| `geom_abline()` / `geom_hline()` / `geom_vline()`                   | `geom-abline`, `geom-hline`, `geom-vline`.            | ✅     | —        | Scalar or array intercepts. Trans-aware: under log10/sqrt, abline samples 64 points so the line follows the warp.       |
| `geom_blank()`                                                      | `geom-blank`.                                         | ✅     | —        | No-op draw; trains scales from its data.                                                                                |
| `geom_function()`                                                   | `geom-function`.                                      | ✅     | —        | Samples a Typst callable across the x-domain into a polyline.                                                           |
| `geom_qq()` / `geom_qq_line()`                                      | `geom-qq`, `geom-qq-line`.                            | ✅     | —        | Standard normal reference distribution; reference line through 25th and 75th sample quantiles.                          |
| `geom_quantile()`                                                   | —                                                     | ⬜     | P4       | Quantile regression; deferred, wasm is last resort.                                                                     |
| `geom_count()`                                                      | —                                                     | ⬜     | P3       | Wrapper over `stat-sum`.                                                                                                |
| `geom_bin_2d()` / `geom_hex()`                                      | —                                                     | ⬜     | P4       | 2D binning.                                                                                                             |
| `geom_contour()` / `geom_contour_filled()`                          | —                                                     | ⬜     | P4       | Marching squares.                                                                                                       |
| `geom_spoke()`                                                      | —                                                     | ⬜     | P4       | Niche.                                                                                                                  |
| `geom_map()` / `geom_sf()` / `geom_sf_label()` / `geom_sf_text()`   | —                                                     | ❌     | ∅        | Maps and sf are out of scope.                                                                                           |

### Stats

| ggplot2                                                                                 | gribouille symbol                   | Status | Priority | Notes                                                                                             |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | ------ | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `stat_identity()`                                                                       | `stat-identity` (implicit).         | ✅     | —        | —                                                                                                 |
| `stat_count()`                                                                          | `stat-count`.                       | ✅     | —        | —                                                                                                 |
| `stat_bin()`                                                                            | `stat-bin`.                         | ✅     | —        | —                                                                                                 |
| `stat_smooth()`                                                                         | `stat-smooth`.                      | 🟡     | P3       | OLS only; `"loess"` deferred, wasm is last resort.                                                |
| `stat_boxplot()`                                                                        | `stat-boxplot`.                     | ✅     | —        | Per-x bucketing with R type-7 quantiles; outliers retained for the geom.                          |
| `stat_summary()` / `stat_summary_bin()`                                                 | `stat-summary`, `stat-summary-bin`. | ✅     | —        | Per-x bucket or per-bin reduction via `mean-se` / `mean-cl-normal` / `mean-sdl` / `median-hilow`. |
| `stat_ecdf()`                                                                           | `stat-ecdf`.                        | ✅     | —        | Empirical cumulative distribution: one row per unique x with `y = count_le_x / n`.                |
| `stat_ellipse()`                                                                        | —                                   | ⬜     | P4       | Normal data ellipses.                                                                             |
| `stat_density()` / `stat_density_2d()` / `stat_density_2d_filled()` / `stat_ydensity()` | —                                   | ⬜     | P4       | KDE; deferred, wasm is last resort.                                                               |
| `stat_contour()` / `stat_contour_filled()`                                              | —                                   | ⬜     | P4       | —                                                                                                 |
| `stat_bin_2d()` / `stat_bin_hex()` / `stat_summary_2d()` / `stat_summary_hex()`         | —                                   | ⬜     | P4       | 2D aggregation.                                                                                   |
| `stat_qq()` / `stat_qq_line()`                                                          | `stat-qq`, `stat-qq-line`.          | ✅     | —        | Reference distribution selectable via `distribution`: `"normal"` (default), `"uniform"`, `"exponential"`. |
| `stat_quantile()`                                                                       | —                                   | ⬜     | P4       | Deferred, wasm is last resort.                                                                    |
| `stat_function()`                                                                       | —                                   | ⬜     | P3       | —                                                                                                 |
| `stat_unique()`                                                                         | `stat-unique`.                      | ✅     | —        | Drops duplicate `(x, y)` rows; first occurrence wins.                                             |
| `stat_manual()` / `stat_align()` / `stat_connect()`                                     | —                                   | ⬜     | P4       | Advanced.                                                                                         |
| `stat_sf()` / `stat_sf_coordinates()`                                                   | —                                   | ❌     | ∅        | Out of scope.                                                                                     |
| `after_stat()` / `after_scale()` / `stage()` / `from_theme()`                           | —                                   | ⬜     | P4       | Late-binding aesthetics.                                                                          |

### Position adjustments

| ggplot2                  | gribouille symbol             | Status | Priority | Notes                                                                                                                                   |
| ------------------------ | ----------------------------- | ------ | -------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `position_identity()`    | implicit default.             | ✅     | —        | —                                                                                                                                       |
| `position_stack()`       | `position-stack`.             | ✅     | —        | Consumed by `geom-col`.                                                                                                                 |
| `position_dodge()`       | `position-dodge`.             | 🟡     | P2       | Uniform-width case implemented; widening to handle variable-width and overlapping marks (the ggplot2 `dodge2` use case) is in Slice 2D. |
| `position_dodge2()`      | merged into `position-dodge`. | ❌     | ∅        | Dropped from the surface; `position-dodge` covers both cases.                                                                           |
| `position_fill()`        | `position-fill`.              | ✅     | —        | Stack normalised to 1 per x bucket.                                                                                                     |
| `position_jitter()`      | `position-jitter`.            | ✅     | —        | Deterministic seed-based offsets; only continuous columns are jittered.                                                                 |
| `position_nudge()`       | `position-nudge`.             | ✅     | —        | Fixed `(x, y)` shift on every row.                                                                                                      |
| `position_jitterdodge()` | —                             | ⬜     | P3       | Dodge then jitter; defer until a real bar+jitter use case appears.                                                                      |

### Annotations

| ggplot2                                       | gribouille symbol | Status | Priority | Notes                                                                                                         |
| --------------------------------------------- | ----------------- | ------ | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `annotate()`                                  | `annotate`.       | ✅     | —        | Sugar over a one-row layer; supports `text`, `point`, `label`, `segment`, `rect`, `vline`, `hline`, `abline`. |
| `annotation_custom()` / `annotation_raster()` | —                 | ⬜     | P3       | Map to CeTZ primitives.                                                                                       |
| `annotation_logticks()`                       | —                 | ⬜     | P3       | Needs log trans first.                                                                                        |
| `annotation_map()` / `borders()`              | —                 | ❌     | ∅        | Maps out of scope.                                                                                            |

### Aesthetics (names consumed by `aes()`)

| ggplot2                                                                    | gribouille                                               | Status | Priority | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- | ------ | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `x`, `y`                                                                   | `x`, `y`.                                                | ✅     | —        | —                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| `xmin`, `xmax`, `ymin`, `ymax`                                             | `xmin`, `xmax`, `ymin`, `ymax`.                          | ✅     | —        | Exposed via `aes()` and trained like other positional aesthetics.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| `xend`, `yend`                                                             | `xend`, `yend`.                                          | ✅     | —        | Folded into the x and y axis domains during scale training; consumed by @geom-segment.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| `colour`, `fill`                                                           | `colour`, `fill`.                                        | ✅     | —        | —                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| `alpha`                                                                    | passed as a constant param.                              | 🟡     | P2       | Promote to a mapped aesthetic.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| `size`                                                                     | via `scale-size-continuous`.                             | 🟡     | P2       | Not mapped via `aes()` in every geom.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| `shape`                                                                    | `shape` via `scale-shape`.                               | ✅     | —        | Circle, square, triangle, diamond, cross, x, star, triangle-down palette.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| `linetype`                                                                 | `linetype` via `scale-linetype`.                         | ✅     | —        | Solid, dashed, dotted, dash-dotted, densely-dashed, loosely-dashed palette.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| `linewidth`                                                                | —                                                        | ⬜     | P2       | Mapped line width.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| `stroke`                                                                   | —                                                        | ⬜     | P3       | Point outline width.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| `group`                                                                    | `group`.                                                 | ✅     | —        | —                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| `weight`                                                                   | —                                                        | ⬜     | P3       | Weighted stats.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| `lower`, `middle`, `upper`, `ymin`, `ymax`, `outliers`                     | computed by `stat-boxplot`, consumed by `geom-boxplot`.  | ✅     | —        | Internal aesthetics carried between stat and geom.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| Inline type coercion (`aes(colour = factor(x))`, `aes(x = as.numeric(g))`) | `as-factor("col")` / `as-numeric("col")` inside `aes()`. | ✅     | —        | ggplot2 lets you force a mapping discrete or continuous at the `aes()` call site with `factor()` / `as.numeric()`. Today gribouille only supports data-level (`as-factor(data, ...)` mutates the column across the plot) or scale-level (`scale-colour-discrete()`) coercion; neither scopes to a single aesthetic, so the same numeric column cannot be continuous on `x` and discrete on `colour` at the same time. Solution: extend the existing `as-factor` and `as-numeric` into dual-arity functions. Called with `(data, col)` they keep their current behaviour; called with `("col")` from inside `aes()` they return a small annotation dict (`(kind: "mapping-ref", var: col, type: "discrete" \| "continuous")`). Update `_column-for-aesthetic` and `_layer-aesthetic-type` in `src/scale/train.typ` to accept either a plain string or the annotation dict. No new public names. |

### Positional scales

| ggplot2                                                                                                                 | gribouille symbol                                                                                       | Status | Priority | Notes                                                                                                                                                    |
| ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `scale_x_continuous()` / `scale_y_continuous()`                                                                         | `scale-x-continuous`, `scale-y-continuous`.                                                             | ✅     | —        | —                                                                                                                                                        |
| `scale_x_discrete()` / `scale_y_discrete()`                                                                             | `scale-x-discrete`, `scale-y-discrete`.                                                                 | ✅     | —        | —                                                                                                                                                        |
| `scale_x_log10()` / `scale_y_log10()`                                                                                   | `scale-x-log10`, `scale-y-log10`.                                                                       | ✅     | —        | Powers-of-10 break placement; sub-decade `(2, 5)` ticks emitted for narrow ranges.                                                                       |
| `scale_x_sqrt()` / `scale_y_sqrt()`                                                                                     | `scale-x-sqrt`, `scale-y-sqrt`.                                                                         | ✅     | —        | —                                                                                                                                                        |
| `scale_x_reverse()` / `scale_y_reverse()`                                                                               | `scale-x-reverse`, `scale-y-reverse`.                                                                   | ✅     | —        | Implemented by swapping the range endpoints during mapping.                                                                                              |
| `scale_x_binned()` / `scale_y_binned()`                                                                                 | —                                                                                                       | ⬜     | P3       | Continuous-to-binned axis.                                                                                                                               |
| `scale_x_date()` / `scale_y_date()` / `scale_x_datetime()` / `scale_y_datetime()` / `scale_x_time()` / `scale_y_time()` | `scale-x-date`, `scale-y-date`, `scale-x-datetime`, `scale-y-datetime`, `scale-x-time`, `scale-y-time`. | ✅     | —        | Accepts ISO-8601 strings (`"2024-01-15"`, `"2024-01-15T08:30:00"`, `"08:30"`) or numeric epoch input. Axis labels formatted via Typst `datetime.display(date-format)`. Timezone offsets ignored. |

### Colour and fill scales

| ggplot2                                                       | gribouille symbol                                                                                    | Status | Priority | Notes                                                                                    |
| ------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `scale_colour_continuous()` / `scale_fill_continuous()`       | `scale-colour-continuous`, `scale-fill-continuous`.                                                  | ✅     | —        | —                                                                                        |
| `scale_colour_discrete()` / `scale_fill_discrete()`           | `scale-colour-discrete`, `scale-fill-discrete`.                                                      | ✅     | —        | —                                                                                        |
| `scale_colour_manual()` / `scale_fill_manual()`               | `scale-colour-manual`, `scale-fill-manual`.                                                          | ✅     | —        | User-supplied palette array.                                                             |
| `scale_colour_viridis_c/d/b()` / `scale_fill_viridis_c/d/b()` | `scale-colour-viridis-c/d/b`, `scale-fill-viridis-c/d/b`.                                            | ✅     | —        | viridis, magma, plasma, inferno, cividis.                                                |
| `scale_colour_brewer()` / `scale_fill_brewer()`               | `scale-colour-brewer`, `scale-fill-brewer`.                                                          | ✅     | —        | 24 ColorBrewer palettes (qualitative, sequential, diverging) via `brewer-palette(name)`. |
| `scale_colour_gradient()` / `gradient2()` / `gradientn()`     | `scale-colour-gradient`, `scale-colour-gradient2`, `scale-colour-gradientn` (and fill counterparts). | ✅     | —        | Two-stop, diverging-around-midpoint, and n-stop gradients; legend colourbars match.      |
| `scale_colour_steps()` / `steps2()` / `stepsn()`              | —                                                                                                    | ⬜     | P3       | Binned gradient.                                                                         |
| `scale_colour_grey()` / `scale_fill_grey()`                   | `scale-colour-grey`, `scale-fill-grey`.                                                              | ✅     | —        | Discrete grey ramp from `start` to `end` luminance.                                      |
| `scale_colour_hue()` / `scale_fill_hue()`                     | `scale-colour-hue`, `scale-fill-hue`.                                                                | ✅     | —        | OKLCh-spaced hues; near-equivalent to ggplot2's HCL defaults.                            |
| `scale_colour_distiller()` / `fermenter()`                    | `scale-colour-distiller`, `scale-fill-distiller`.                                                    | ✅     | —        | Continuous Brewer; `direction: -1` reverses. `fermenter` (binned) deferred.              |
| `scale_colour_identity()` / `scale_fill_identity()`           | `scale-colour-identity`, `scale-fill-identity`.                                                      | ✅     | —        | Column values are passed through (hex strings or `color` values). No legend is drawn.    |

### Other aesthetic scales

| ggplot2                                                                                                                                  | gribouille symbol                                  | Status | Priority | Notes                                                    |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------- | ------ | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| `scale_alpha()` / `*_continuous()` / `*_discrete()`                                                                                      | —                                                  | ⬜     | P2       | After `alpha` aesthetic lands.                           |
| `scale_size()` / `scale_radius()`                                                                                                        | `scale-size-continuous`.                           | 🟡     | P2       | Add radius variant.                                      |
| `scale_size_area()` / `scale_size_binned_area()`                                                                                         | —                                                  | ⬜     | P2       | Area-proportional sizing.                                |
| `scale_size_binned()`                                                                                                                    | —                                                  | ⬜     | P3       | —                                                        |
| `scale_shape()` / `scale_shape_manual()` / `scale_shape_binned()`                                                                        | `scale-shape`, `scale-shape-manual`.               | 🟡     | P3       | `_binned` variant deferred.                              |
| `scale_linetype()` / `scale_linetype_manual()` / `scale_linetype_binned()` / `scale_linetype_continuous()` / `scale_linetype_discrete()` | `scale-linetype`, `scale-linetype-manual`.         | 🟡     | P3       | `_binned`, `_continuous`, `_discrete` variants deferred. |
| `scale_linewidth()` / `scale_linewidth_manual()` / `scale_linewidth_binned()`                                                            | —                                                  | ⬜     | P2       | After `linewidth` aesthetic.                             |
| `scale_shape_identity()` / `scale_linetype_identity()`                                                                                   | `scale-shape-identity`, `scale-linetype-identity`. | ✅     | —        | Column values used as the shape / dash keyword directly. |
| `scale_size_identity()` / `scale_alpha_identity()` / `scale_linewidth_identity()`                                                        | —                                                  | ⬜     | P2       | Land alongside the matching aesthetic promotion.         |

### Labels and limits

| ggplot2                           | gribouille symbol                                                 | Status | Priority | Notes                                                                               |
| --------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `labs()`                          | `labs(title:, subtitle:, caption:, x:, y:, colour:, fill:, ...)`. | ✅     | —        | Axis names are injected into trained scale specs.                                   |
| `xlab()` / `ylab()` / `ggtitle()` | `xlab`, `ylab`, `ggtitle`.                                        | ✅     | —        | Thin wrappers over `labs()`.                                                        |
| `get_labs()`                      | —                                                                 | ⬜     | P3       | —                                                                                   |
| `lims()` / `xlim()` / `ylim()`    | `lims`, `xlim`, `ylim`.                                           | ✅     | —        | Sugar over `scale-x-continuous(limits:)` / `scale-y-continuous(limits:)`.           |
| `expansion()` / `expand_scale()`  | via scale `expand:`.                                              | 🟡     | P3       | Expose helper.                                                                      |
| `expand_limits()`                 | `expand-limits`.                                                  | ✅     | —        | Folds extension values into the trained continuous domain rather than replacing it. |

### Guides

| ggplot2                                                        | gribouille symbol                               | Status | Priority | Notes                                                                 |
| -------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- | ------ | -------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Discrete `guide_legend()` for colour/fill                      | automatic legend.                               | ✅     | —        | —                                                                     |
| `guide_colourbar()` / `guide_colorbar()`                       | automatic colourbar.                            | ✅     | —        | Emitted when trained colour/fill is continuous.                       |
| `guide_legend()` customisation (nrow, ncol, reverse, ...)      | `guide-legend(title:, nrow:, ncol:, reverse:)`. | ✅     | —        | Plot-level binding via `guides(colour: guide-legend(...))`.           |
| `guide_bins()` / `guide_coloursteps()`                         | —                                               | ⬜     | P3       | After binned scales.                                                  |
| `guide_axis()`                                                 | `guide-axis(angle:, n-dodge:)`.                 | ✅     | —        | x-axis only for v1; y-axis rotation/dodge deferred.                   |
| `guide_axis_logticks()`                                        | —                                               | ⬜     | P3       | After log trans.                                                      |
| `guide_none()`                                                 | `guide-none`.                                   | ✅     | —        | Suppress a specific aesthetic's legend.                               |
| `guide_axis_stack()` / `guide_axis_theta()` / `guide_custom()` | —                                               | ⬜     | P4       | Advanced.                                                             |
| `guides()` top-level                                           | `guides`.                                       | ✅     | —        | Threaded into `plot(..., guides: guides(colour: guide-legend(...)))`. |
| `sec_axis()` / `dup_axis()` / `derive()`                       | —                                               | ⬜     | P3       | Secondary axes.                                                       |
| `draw_key_*()` glyph family                                    | —                                               | ⬜     | P3       | Per-geom legend glyphs.                                               |

### Facetting

| ggplot2                                                                                                                        | gribouille symbol           | Status | Priority | Notes                                                                                                     |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------- | ------ | -------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `facet_wrap()`                                                                                                                 | `facet-wrap`.               | ✅     | —        | `scales = "fixed"` (default), `"free"`, `"free_x"`, `"free_y"`; per-facet stats via `partition-by-group`. |
| `facet_grid()`                                                                                                                 | `facet-grid(rows:, cols:)`. | ✅     | —        | Strips on top (columns) and right (rows); shared scales only.                                             |
| `vars()`                                                                                                                       | —                           | ⬜     | P2       | Helper to quote faceting variables.                                                                       |
| `labeller()` / `label_value()` / `label_both()` / `label_context()` / `label_parsed()` / `label_wrap_gen()` / `label_bquote()` | —                           | ⬜     | P3       | Strip label customisation.                                                                                |

### Coordinates

| ggplot2                                           | gribouille symbol  | Status | Priority | Notes                                                                                                                                                              |
| ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------ | ------ | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `coord_cartesian()`                               | `coord-cartesian`. | ✅     | —        | `xlim`/`ylim` override trained x/y domains (data is not dropped; out-of-range marks still render).                                                                 |
| `coord_fixed()`                                   | `coord-fixed`.     | ✅     | —        | Locks the aspect ratio so one x data unit equals `ratio` y data units. Honoured in non-faceted, facet-wrap (per-panel under free scales), and facet-grid renders.           |
| `coord_flip()`                                    | —                  | ⬜     | P2       | Swap x and y.                                                                                                                                                      |
| `coord_polar()` / `coord_radial()`                | —                  | ⬜     | P4       | Significant rewrite.                                                                                                                                               |
| `coord_trans()` / `coord_transform()`             | —                  | ⬜     | P4       | Visual transformation without scale retraining.                                                                                                                    |
| `coord_map()` / `coord_quickmap()` / `coord_sf()` | —                  | ❌     | ∅        | Maps out of scope.                                                                                                                                                 |

### Themes

| ggplot2                                                                                                                                 | gribouille symbol                                                                      | Status | Priority | Notes                                                                  |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `theme_minimal()`                                                                                                                       | `theme-minimal`.                                                                       | ✅     | —        | —                                                                      |
| `theme_classic()`                                                                                                                       | `theme-classic`.                                                                       | ✅     | —        | —                                                                      |
| `theme_void()`                                                                                                                          | `theme-void`.                                                                          | ✅     | —        | —                                                                      |
| `theme_grey()` / `theme_bw()` / `theme_linedraw()` / `theme_light()` / `theme_dark()` / `theme_test()`                                  | `theme-grey`, `theme-bw`, `theme-linedraw`, `theme-light`, `theme-dark`, `theme-test`. | ✅     | —        | British spelling only: no `theme-gray` alias.                          |
| `theme()`                                                                                                                               | `theme(..fields)`.                                                                     | ✅     | —        | Translates `element_*` dicts into flat keys consumed by `merge-theme`. |
| `element_blank()` / `element_rect()` / `element_line()` / `element_text()` / `element_polygon()` / `element_point()` / `element_geom()` | `element-blank`, `element-rect`, `element-line`, `element-text`.                       | 🟡     | P2       | `element-polygon`/`element-point`/`element-geom` deferred.             |
| `margin()` / `margin_part()` / `margin_auto()` / `rel()`                                                                                | —                                                                                      | ⬜     | P3       | Sizing helpers.                                                        |
| `theme_get()` / `theme_set()` / `theme_update()` / `theme_replace()` / `%+replace%`                                                     | —                                                                                      | ⬜     | P3       | Global theme state; may stay plot-arg only.                            |
| `theme_sub_axis*()` / `theme_sub_legend()` / `theme_sub_panel()` / `theme_sub_plot()` / `theme_sub_strip()`                             | —                                                                                      | ⬜     | P4       | Shortcut groups.                                                       |

### Vector helpers

| ggplot2                                                            | gribouille symbol                                        | Status | Priority | Notes                                                               |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------- | ------ | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `cut_interval()` / `cut_number()` / `cut_width()`                  | `cut-interval`, `cut-number`, `cut-width`.               | ✅     | —        | Pure helpers; bin labels of the form `"(lo,hi]"`.                   |
| `resolution()`                                                     | `resolution`.                                            | ✅     | —        | Zero-aware by default; matches ggplot2.                             |
| `mean_se()` / `mean_cl_normal()` / `mean_sdl()` / `median_hilow()` | `mean-se`, `mean-cl-normal`, `mean-sdl`, `median-hilow`. | ✅     | —        | `mean-cl-normal` panics on `conf != 0.95`. `mean_cl_boot` deferred. |

### Programming and extension

| ggplot2                                                                          | gribouille symbol | Status | Priority | Notes                                                             |
| -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------- | ------ | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `ggproto()` / `ggproto_parent()`                                                 | —                 | ❌     | ∅        | S3/R6 pattern; Typst uses dict-of-functions instead.              |
| `aes_()` / `aes_string()` / `aes_q()`                                            | —                 | ❌     | ∅        | Deprecated upstream.                                              |
| `print.ggplot` / `plot.ggplot`                                                   | —                 | ❌     | ∅        | Typst handles rendering.                                          |
| `autoplot()` / `autolayer()` / `fortify()` / `map_data()` / `automatic_plotting` | —                 | ❌     | ∅        | R S3 dispatch.                                                    |
| `get_alt_text()`                                                                 | `get-alt-text`.   | ✅     | —        | Reads `spec.alt` set via the new `plot(..., alt: ...)` parameter. |

### Bundled datasets

| ggplot2                                                                                                                              | gribouille                              | Status | Priority | Notes                                                               |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------- | ------ | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `mpg`, `economics`                                                                                                                   | `mpg` (30 rows), `economics` (24 rows). | ✅     | —        | Small subsets bundled for examples; values plausible but not exact. |
| Other datasets (`diamonds`, `economics_long`, `faithfuld`, `midwest`, `msleep`, `presidential`, `seals`, `txhousing`, `luv_colours`) | —                                       | ⬜     | P4       | Ship if a real example asks for them.                               |

## Coverage at a glance

- **Geoms**: 11 of ~35 implemented (`point`, `line`, `col`, `bar`, `histogram`, `ribbon`, `smooth`, `hline`, `vline`, `abline`, `text`, `label`), 1 stubbed (`boxplot`); rest missing or out of scope.
- **Stats**: 4 of ~18 implemented.
- **Positions**: 3 of 7 (identity, stack, dodge, fill).
- **Scales**: position, discrete/continuous/manual/viridis colour and fill, size, shape, linetype — ~15 symbols; binned variants deferred.
- **Coords**: `coord-cartesian` with xlim/ylim; others missing.
- **Facets**: `facet-wrap` (with `fixed`/`free`/`free_x`/`free_y` scales) and `facet-grid` (shared scales only).
- **Themes**: 3 complete themes plus `theme()` + four `element-*` constructors.
- **Guides**: discrete swatch legend plus colourbar for continuous colour/fill.

## How this page is updated

Every implementation slice flips the Status column for every row it touches in the same commit as the code.
The orchestrator owns these edits.
See the development roadmap under `.claude/dev-plans/` for the slice ordering and protocol.
